title: Bonellia paludicola
taxonomy: Bonellia paludicola is classified within the kingdom Animalia, with the phylum Nematoda being its most specific classification at the phylum level. This classification places Bonellia paludicola among the roundworms, a group of multicellular, bilaterally symmetrical animals. The species Bonellia paludicola is further classified within the class Chromadorea, which includes various types of nematode worms. Within the class Chromadorea, Bonellia paludicola is classified as a member of the order Adenophorea, a group of nematodes that are primarily free-living. Bonellia paludicola is a marine species, and its classification within the order Adenophorea is consistent with its free-living lifestyle.

intro: Bonellia paludicola, commonly known as Bonellia, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the genus Bonellia and family Zosterophyllaceae. The health benefits of Bonellia include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the management of conditions such as arthritis and oxidative stress.
